2604.26274.title: Enforcing Benign Trajectories: A Behavioral Firewall for Structured-Workflow AI Agents
2604.26274.paper_summary: Structured-workflow agents driven by large language models execute tool calls against sensitive external environments. We propose \codename, a telemetry-driven behavioral anomaly detection firewall. Drawing on sequence-based intrusion detection, \codename\ compiles verified benign tool-call telemetry into a parameterized deterministic finite automaton (pDFA). The model defines permitted tool sequences, sequential contexts, and parameter bounds. At runtime, a lightweight gateway enforces these boundaries via an $O(1)$ state-transition structural lookup, shifting computationally expensive analysis entirely offline. Evaluated on the Agent Security Bench (ASB), \codename\ achieves a 5.6\% macro-averaged attack success rate (ASR) across five scenarios. Within three structured workflows, ASR drops to 2.2\%, outperforming Aegis, a state-of-the-art stateless scanner, at 12.8\%. \codename\ achieves 0\% ASR on multi-step and context-sequential attacks in structured settings. Furthermore, against 1,000 algorithmically spliced exfiltration payloads, only 1.4\% matched valid structural paths, all of which failed end-to-end string parameter guards (0 successes out of 14 surviving paths, 95\% CI [0\%, 23.2\%]). \codename\ introduces just 2.2~ms of per-call latency (a 3.7$\times$ speedup over \textsc{Aegis}) while maintaining a 2.0\% benign task failure rate (BTFR) on benign workloads. Modeling the behavioral trajectory effectively collapses the available attack surface, but unmaintained continuous parameter bounds remain vulnerable to synonym-substitution attacks (18\% evasion rate). Thus, exact-match whitelisting of sensitive parameters ultimately bears the final defensive load against execution.
2604.26274.paper_summary_zh: 結構化工作流程代理由大型語言模型驅動，對敏感的外部環境執行工具調用。 我們提出了\codename，一個基於遙測的行為異常檢測防火牆。 依據基於序列的入侵檢測，\codename\ 將經過驗證的良性工具調用遙測編譯成參數化的確定性有限自動機（pDFA）。 該模型定義了允許的工具序列、序列上下文和參數範圍。 在運行時，輕量級網關通過$O(1)$狀態轉換結構查找來強制執行這些邊界，將計算密集型分析完全移至離線。 在Agent Security Bench（ASB）上的評估中，\codename\ 在五個場景中實現了5.6\%的宏觀平均攻擊成功率（ASR）。 在三個結構化工作流程中，ASR降至2.2\%，超越了Aegis，這是一個最先進的無狀態掃描器，達到12.8\%。 \codename\ 在結構化環境中的多步驟和上下文序列攻擊上實現了0\%的ASR。此外，對於1,000個算法拼接的外洩有效載荷，只有1.4\%匹配有效的結構路徑，所有這些都未能通過端到端字符串參數保護（在14條存活路徑中0次成功，95\% CI [0\%, 23.2\%]）。 \codename\ 每次調用僅引入2.2~毫秒的延遲（相較於\textsc{Aegis}加速3.7$\times$），同時在良性工作負載上保持2.0\%的良性任務失敗率（BTFR）。 有效建模行為軌跡確實縮小了可用的攻擊面，但未維護的連續參數範圍仍然容易受到同義詞替換攻擊（18\%的躲避率）。 因此，對敏感參數的精確匹配白名單最終承擔了對執行的最終防禦負擔。
2604.26274.author: Hung Dang et.al.
